name: guardrails
description: Rule-based PreToolUse hooks that block noisy Bash habits: static echoes, shelling out past dedicated tools, stacked commands, and backgrounding.

bash_no_static_echo.title: Block static echo
bash_no_static_echo.description: Deny echo of static text (banners, status words, labels); echo of dynamic input like $VAR or $(cmd) is allowed.

bash_no_redundant_echo.title: Block redundant echo
bash_no_redundant_echo.description: Deny echoing a command's output (echo $(cmd) or echo `cmd`); run the command directly instead.

bash_no_backgrounding.title: Block backgrounding
bash_no_backgrounding.description: Deny commands backgrounded with & instead of the run_in_background parameter.

bash_shelling_out.title: Prefer dedicated tools
bash_shelling_out.description: Warn when a command shells out to grep, sed, find, cat, head, or tail.

bash_stacking.title: Warn on stacked commands
bash_stacking.description: Warn when independent commands are joined with ; instead of running as parallel tool calls.
